title: Use of Funds Operator Lesson
objective: Understand the operator fundamentals behind use of funds in capital / fundraising.
beginnerExplanation: Use of Funds becomes easier when you break it into signal, decisions, owners, and follow-through. This lesson gives you a practical structure instead of abstract advice.
deeperExplanation: At higher levels, use of funds is about tradeoffs. You are deciding where to focus, what to ignore, and how to keep the work legible enough for other people to trust.
keyTakeaways: Use of Funds improves when the operating goal is explicit.
You need evidence, not just motion, to judge whether use of funds is working.
Use of Funds should leave behind reusable assets like notes, decisions, scorecards, or systems.
example: A founder or operator uses use of funds to clarify what changed, what matters next, and what must happen before the next review.
commonMistakes: Treating use of funds like isolated activity instead of part of a larger system.
Skipping the review loop and assuming the first answer is good enough.
Letting ownership stay vague after the decision is made.
exercise: Write a one-page note explaining how you would improve use of funds over the next two weeks inside a real business.
